title: Toroidal Wrapping, Object Bounds, Viewports, Resize, and Scaling · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.
Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Compare shortest toroidal displacement or ghost proxies so collisions remain continuous across seams.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Center Vs Bounds
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 6379 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. # Evidence: asteroids-resize-dpi-d383sb
# Competency: asteroids-resize-dpi.
# Case 2100: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_resize_dpi_d383sb(x: float = -4.0, y: float = 605.0, width: float = 800.0, height: float = 600.0, radius: float = 12.0, resize_preserves_world: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_d383sb = "d383sb-bs383d-2100"
    assert evidence_variant_d383sb
    evidence_axes_d383sb = (
        "playerd383sb",
        "seed2100",
        "inputbs383d",
        "framed383sb2100",
        "state2100d383sb",
        "playtestbs383d2100",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_d383sb)) == 6
    if width <= 0.0 or height <= 0.0 or radius < 0.0:
        return False, width, "viewport-domain"
    wrapped_x = ((x + radius) % (width + 2 * radius)) - radius
    wrapped_y = ((y + radius) % (height + 2 * radius)) - radius
    if not resize_preserves_world:
        return False, wrapped_x, "resize-teleport"
    if not (-radius <= wrapped_x < width + radius):
        return False, wrapped_x, "wrap-x"
    assert -radius <= wrapped_y < height + radius
    return True, round(wrapped_x, 3), "seam-safe-wrap"
